own_review_points: array_sort with literal ASC/DESC NULLS FIRST/LAST suffix must match actual DB grammar (HSQL sort_array, PG) - emulation correctness
Oracle generated PL/SQL marked deterministic while doing dynamic table(arr) SQL - may be invalid/optimizer hazard
NULLS-placement default = sort direction default per docs; verify rendering matches in expression (parameter) path vs literal path
needsArrayCasting only true for string types - numeric array coalesce(cast(array[] as TYPE)) fallback element-type mismatch
H2 emulation bounded by getMaximumArraySize() (1000) - silent truncation for large arrays
PostgreSQL/H2 array_sort emulation could be simplified the same way HSQL was (human ref)
notes: Only the Oracle 'deterministic' point is a substantive concrete concern (good). The rest are hedged 'verify/confirm/worth noting' observations that restate the diff without asserting a defect, and the maximumArraySize point self-concludes 'acceptable'. It missed the reviewer's actual feedback - that the H2 and PostgreSQL emulations could be simplified to match the HSQL approach. Net slightly negative: many trivial, two missed.
